page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-16 is a compute-focused virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The instance is configured with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1.0 GB per core ratio. This hardware profile does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results position the n2-highcpu-16 in the average tier across most standardized tests. The CPU delivers a stress-ng single-core performance of 1408.59 and a multi-core score of 14867.36. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block bandwidth of 828.44 GB/sec, which drops to 82.43 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write bandwidth of 42.68 GB/sec. While multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak at 35.09 GB/sec, other operations like Redis SET achieve an average of 14.12 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 12.3 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-highcpu-16 provides a dense allocation of dedicated compute resources relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for CPU-bound tasks that do not require large memory allocations. The inclusion of AVX-512 vector extensions supports optimized mathematical and cryptographic operations. This instance is suited for workloads such as software compilation, web serving, lightweight serialization tasks, and small-scale language model inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-16 is an x86_64 compute-focused server instance featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz. Lacking bundled local storage and GPU accelerators, this instance is tailored for CPU-bound workloads. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most metrics, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 7164.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 15418.2. Its memory bandwidth reaches 828.44 GB/sec for small cached blocks but decreases to 82.43 GB/sec for larger uncached blocks. With a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the instance offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-heavy tasks that do not demand massive memory footprints. It is well-suited for applications such as software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-16 is a compute-focused instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering balanced mid-tier performance for CPU-bound tasks.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-16 features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 16-vCPU compute instance with a 1:1 memory-to-core ratio, optimized for processing-heavy workloads without local storage overhead.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory
Intel x86_64 architecture with AVX-512 instruction support
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Average performance in multi-core processing and web serving benchmarks
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight database tasks
